channel.title: dotnet/runtimelab Issues
channel.description: Issues from https://github.com/dotnet/runtimelab, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: Issue: Analyzer with codefixes/quick actions - @kant2002
item.description:
I'm a bit tired of changing a lot of code to support NativeAOT I mean

  • Enum.GetValues(typeof(T)) => Enum.GetValues<T>()
  • Marshal.SizeOf(typeof(T)) => Marshal.SizeOf<T>()
  • Marshal.PtrToStructure(IntPtr, typeof(T)) => Marshal.PtrToStructure<T>(IntPtr) (I did not try, but assume this change is also required) and I believe it maybe more in future. I thinking about Analyzer with code fixes which may do these replacements. I remember that there already analyzers which helps to report about incorrect patterns as mentioned here https://github.com/dotnet/runtimelab/pull/666#issuecomment-776555938.

So question where this is located, and is that location is good fit for this kind of change?

item.title: Issue: [NativeAOT] undefined references when using static linking - @andreakarasho
item.description:
macOS Mojave 10.14

           "___isPlatformVersionAtLeast", referenced from:
               _AppleCryptoNative_EccGenerateKey in libSystem.Security.Cryptography.Native.Apple.a(pal_ecc.c.o)
               _AppleCryptoNative_RsaGenerateKey in libSystem.Security.Cryptography.Native.Apple.a(pal_rsa.c.o)
           "_kSecUseDataProtectionKeychain", referenced from:
               _AppleCryptoNative_EccGenerateKey in libSystem.Security.Cryptography.Native.Apple.a(pal_ecc.c.o)
               _AppleCryptoNative_RsaGenerateKey in libSystem.Security.Cryptography.Native.Apple.a(pal_rsa.c.o)

Tried to add <NativeLibrary Include="-framework Security" /> but did not help

item.title: Issue: Failure to compile MsQuicApi class - @kant2002
item.description:
I hit that in WinForms app, where is no networking, so I assume this is PictureBox being impolite.

D:\d\kant\GitHub\dotnet\runtimelab\src\coreclr\jit\importer.cpp:20927
D:\d\kant\GitHub\dotnet\runtimelab\src\coreclr\jit\importer.cpp:20927
Assertion failed '((size_t)exactContext & CORINFO_CONTEXTFLAGS_MASK) == CORINFO_CONTEXTFLAGS_CLASS' in 'System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.Internal.MsQuicApi:.cctor()' during 'Importation' (IL size 259)

Assertion failed '((size_t)exactContext & CORINFO_CONTEXTFLAGS_MASK) == CORINFO_CONTEXTFLAGS_CLASS' in 'System.Net.Quic.Implementations.MsQuic.Internal.SafeMsQuicConfigurationHandle:.cctor()' during 'Importation' (IL size 55)

item.title: Issue: NativeAOT-LLVM: Question: Should ints < 32 bits be used as parameter types? - @yowl
item.description:
In the IL-> LLVM code a function that returns a bool like https://github.com/dotnet/runtimelab/blob/482b83e1272e8225410c2056d846bd3919557d4e/src/coreclr/nativeaot/System.Private.CoreLib/src/Internal/Runtime/Augments/EnumInfo.cs#L63

will have an LLVM return type of i1. For the RyuJIT->LLVM module this is a bit of a problem as ints are 32 bits so the above method gets compilled to

define i1 @S_P_CoreLib_Internal_Runtime_Augments_EnumInfo__get_HasFlagsAttribute(i8* %0) {
Prolog:
  br label %1

1:                                                ; preds = %Prolog
  %2 = bitcast i8* %0 to i8**
  %3 = load i8*, i8** %2, align 4
  %4 = getelementptr i8, i8* %3, i32 20
  %CastPtrToPtr = bitcast i8* %4 to i32*
  %5 = load i32, i32* %CastPtrToPtr, align 4
  ret i32 %5
}

Which does not compile as ret i32 does not match the define i1 . Would it be more inline with RyuJIT IR to use i32 for all parameter and return types of i8, i16, i32?

item.title: Issue: ResourceManager not working in reflection-free mode - @kant2002
item.description:
I try to run simplest reading from resources using codegen from Resx files.

With this configuration

<ItemGroup>
  <RuntimeHostConfigurationOption Include="Switch.System.Reflection.Assembly.SimulatedLocationInBaseDirectory" Value="true" />
  <RuntimeHostConfigurationOption Include="Switch.System.Reflection.Disabled.DoNotThrowForNames" Value="true" />
  <RuntimeHostConfigurationOption Include="Switch.System.Reflection.Disabled.DoNotThrowForAssembly" Value="true" />
</ItemGroup>

It fails at this line

https://github.com/dotnet/runtimelab/blob/b4c3760e16d8add69df5e5ae4ed6ac18aa3098b4/src/coreclr/nativeaot/System.Private.CoreLib/src/System/Resources/ManifestBasedResourceGroveler.CoreRT.cs#L21

with NotImplementedException.

My massage skills fails at this point.

item.title: Issue: Can `typeof(T).Module` be supported in reflection-free mode? - @kant2002
item.description:
It looks similar to typeof(T).Assembly and I think some limited form of object can be constructed. Example of usage

https://github.com/dotnet/winforms/blob/f3c6d29f74c23eb773cfaa7efebfa1eee5635b98/src/System.Windows.Forms/src/System/Windows/Forms/Application.cs#L827-L828

item.title: Issue: More usable reflection-free mode - @kant2002
item.description:
I notice that https://github.com/dotnet/corert/issues/8216 indicating that this may be available in NativeAOT context. With that method available, or even better Enum.IsDefined, WinForms can be usable in reflection-free mode.

There 19 usages of that method in WinForms, some of them in assertions, some in tests, but couple in legit places which guard public API, so cannot be easily removed/replaced.

item.title: Issue: NullabilityInfoContextTests failing in NativeAOT - @jkotas
Issue: Cannot box a ByRef-like (ref struct) when using a CreateDelegate method on method that accepts a ByRef-like parameter. - @Qapples
item.description:
The following code correctly runs on CoreCLR and fails on NativeAOT: https://gist.github.com/Suchiman/b09ad481065e3e2b17d89e321121bacc

NativeAOT fails with the following exception: System.NotSupportedException: Cannot create boxed ByRef-like values.

The issue occurs at this line: typeof(Program).GetMethod("Method")!.CreateDelegate<ExampleDelegate>();

item.title: Issue: Occasional SEHException in ObjWriter - @kant2002
item.description:
I'm occasionally hit exceptions in ObjWriter. By occasion I mean maybe after update to nightlies, or after I do some modifications in application.

EXEC : error : External component has thrown an exception. 
  System.Runtime.InteropServices.SEHException (0x80004005): External component has thrown an exception.
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.FinishObjWriter(IntPtr objWriter)
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.Dispose(Boolean bDisposing) in ILCompiler.Compiler.dll:token 0x6000dd5+0x12
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.EmitObject(String objectFilePath, IEnumerable`1 nodes, NodeFactory factory, ObjectWritingOptions options, IObjectDumper dumper) in ILCompiler.Compiler.dll:token 0x6000dda+0x367
     at ILCompiler.RyuJitCompilation.CompileInternal(String outputFile, ObjectDumper dumper) in ILCompiler.RyuJit.dll:token 0x600022b+0x35
     at ILCompiler.Compilation.ILCompiler.ICompilation.Compile(String outputFile, ObjectDumper dumper) in ILCompiler.Compiler.dll:token 0x6000322+0x9
     at ILCompiler.Program.Run(String[] args) in ilc.dll:token 0x600010d+0xc7e
     at ILCompiler.Program.Main(String[] args) in ilc.dll:token 0x6000113+0x5

or

 Fatal error. System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to read or write protected memory. This is often an indication that other memory is corrupt.
  Repeat 2 times:
  --------------------------------
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.FinishObjWriter(IntPtr)
  --------------------------------
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.Dispose(Boolean)
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.EmitObject(System.String, System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysisFramework.DependencyNode>, ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.NodeFactory, ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWritingOptions, ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.IObjectDumper)
     at ILCompiler.RyuJitCompilation.CompileInternal(System.String, ILCompiler.ObjectDumper)
     at ILCompiler.Compilation.ILCompiler.ICompilation.Compile(System.String, ILCompiler.ObjectDumper)
     at ILCompiler.Program.Run(System.String[])
     at ILCompiler.Program.Main(System.String[])

My problem when I hit this error, I have no idea what's going on, and how to intercept SEH to at least understand what method is offending codegen. What is worrying me is that this two errors appear on same inputs. And prior that, I have an issue and on next day it solve itself.

Questions:

  • Is there a way how I can intercept SEH in C# code, so I at least know what method produce error.
  • Is there a way how make ObjWriter to not blow up on some issues?

item.title: Issue: [NativeAOT] ModuleInitializer does not work - @andreakarasho
item.description:
using System;
using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;

namespace Test
{
    class Program
    {
        public static int _counter = 0;
            
        
        public static void Main()
        {
            Console.WriteLine($"Hello world {++_counter}");
        }
		
	[ModuleInitializer]
	public static void Init()
	{
	     _counter = 999;
	}
    }
}

output:

Hello world 1

item.title: Issue: NativeAOT:How to fix MongoDB.Driver infinite generic expansion? - @loyldg
item.description: I am using MongoDB.Driver(Clone code to local),and have two problems in AOT compilation. 1.Catching generic exceptions 2.Infinite generic cycle,mentioned at this link https://github.com/dotnet/corert/issues/8232#issuecomment-660278463 The first issue can be fixed using exception filter "catch (Exception ex) when (ex is T)" I don't know how to fix the second one,any suggestions?

item.title: Issue: Enable FEATURE_GENERIC_MATH - @jkotas
item.description: Depends on static virtual methods - https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/54063

item.title: Issue: CMD mentioned when compile to linux-musl-x64 - @kant2002
item.description:
I try to publish to linux-musl-x64 on my Gentoo with only glibc, and hit following error message

/tmp/tmp8934f0fcf1a94aeeb5d8e46168255ccd.exec.cmd: line 2: ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-musl-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21352.1/tools/ilc: No such file or directory

item.title: Issue: Crash of ILC - @kant2002
item.description:
I attempt to compile https://github.com/MicrosoftEdge/WebView2Samples/tree/master/SampleApps/WebView2WindowsFormsBrowser with only exception that I retarget to net6.0 and nothing more.

  Fatal error. System.AccessViolationException: Attempted to read or write protected memory. This is often an indication that other memory is corrupt.
  Repeat 2 times:
  --------------------------------
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.EmitSymbolRef(IntPtr, Byte[], ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.RelocType, Int32)
  --------------------------------
     at ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWriter.EmitObject(System.String, System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable`1<ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysisFramework.DependencyNode>, ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.NodeFactory, ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.ObjectWritingOptions, ILCompiler.DependencyAnalysis.IObjectDumper)
     at ILCompiler.RyuJitCompilation.CompileInternal(System.String, ILCompiler.ObjectDumper)
     at ILCompiler.Compilation.ILCompiler.ICompilation.Compile(System.String, ILCompiler.ObjectDumper)
     at ILCompiler.Program.Run(System.String[])
     at ILCompiler.Program.Main(System.String[])

ILC version 6.0.0-preview.7.21351. dotnet SDK 6.0.100-preview.6.21315.23 Microsoft.AspNetCore.App 6.0.0-preview.6.21306.3 Microsoft.NETCore.App 6.0.0-preview.6.21306.1 Microsoft.WindowsDesktop.App 6.0.0-preview.6.21305.3

I realise that I again mismatch runtime versions.

item.title: Issue: StaticLinking failed locally and in Docker - @kant2002
item.description:
I attempt to replicate https://github.com/dotnet/corert/issues/7510

And Docker image build fine, but during running container crashed.

$ docker run test
docker: Error response from daemon: OCI runtime create failed: container_linux.go:380: starting container process caused: exec: "/app/HelloWorld": stat /app/HelloWorld: no such file or directory: unknown.

So I decide to run locally on my Gentoo, and it fails.

  /usr/bin/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-ld: ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/sdk/libRuntime.a(gcenv.unix.cpp.o): in function `NUMASupportInitialize()':
  /__w/1/s/src/coreclr/gc/unix/gcenv.unix.cpp:301: warning: Using 'dlopen' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
  /usr/bin/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-ld: cannot find -lz
  /usr/bin/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-ld: ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Native.a(pal_uid.c.o): in function `SystemNative_GetGroupList':
  /__w/1/s/src/libraries/Native/Unix/System.Native/pal_uid.c:212: warning: Using 'getgrouplist' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
  /usr/bin/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-ld: ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Native.a(pal_uid.c.o): in function `SystemNative_GetPwNamR':
  /__w/1/s/src/libraries/Native/Unix/System.Native/pal_uid.c:85: warning: Using 'getpwnam_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
  /usr/bin/x86_64-pc-linux-gnu-ld: ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Native.a(pal_uid.c.o): in function `SystemNative_GetPwUidR':
  /__w/1/s/src/libraries/Native/Unix/System.Native/pal_uid.c:68: warning: Using 'getpwuid_r' in statically linked applications requires at runtime the shared libraries from the glibc version used for linking
clang-12 : error : linker command failed with exit code 1 (use -v to see invocation) [~/d/NativeAOTDocker/HelloWorldStatic.csproj]
~/.nuget/packages/microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/build/Microsoft.NETCore.Native.targets(349,5): error MSB3073: The command "clang "obj/Release/net5.0/linux-x64/native/HelloWorldStatic.o" -o "bin/Release/net5.0/linux-x64/native/HelloWorldStatic" ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/sdk/libbootstrapper.a ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/sdk/libRuntime.a ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Native.a ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Globalization.Native.a ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.IO.Compression.Native.a ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Net.Security.Native.a ~/.nuget/packages/runtime.linux-x64.microsoft.dotnet.ilcompiler/6.0.0-preview.7.21327.1/framework/libSystem.Security.Cryptography.Native.OpenSsl.a -g -Wl,-rpath,'$ORIGIN' -Wl,--as-needed -pthread -lstdc++ -ldl -lm -lz -static -lrt -Wl,--discard-all -Wl,--gc-sections" exited with code 1. [~/d/NativeAOTDocker/HelloWorldStatic.csproj]

This seems to be separate issues, but I decide to record that anyway to be aware about the problem. Will try to look at Docker issue later today.

item.title: Issue: Can't get stack traces when reflection disabled - @MichalStrehovsky
item.description:
Broke this in dotnet/runtimelab#1248.

See https://github.com/MichalStrehovsky/bflat/issues/7

item.title: Issue: OS dev with this? - @Shadowblitz16
item.description: Is it possible to compile a .net 5 application to bare metal x86-64 hardware with this?

item.title: Issue: FailFast doesn't fail fast enough - @MichalStrehovsky
item.description:
We hit a segfault when ICU is missing on Linux instead of the helpful FailFast message ("Couldn't find a valid ICU package installed on the system.").

We should probably short-circuit Console.Error and do file writes to stderr directly.

Thread 1 "bflat" received signal SIGSEGV, Segmentation fault.
0x0000000001a03895 in System_Console_System_Text_EncodingHelper__GetCharset ()
(gdb) bt
#0  0x0000000001a03895 in System_Console_System_Text_EncodingHelper__GetCharset ()
#1  0x0000000001a037a9 in System_Console_System_Text_EncodingHelper__GetEncodingFromCharset ()
#2  0x00000000019fd729 in System_Console_System_ConsolePal__GetConsoleEncoding ()
#3  0x00000000019fc738 in System_Console_System_Console__get_OutputEncoding ()
#4  0x00000000019fc80b in System_Console_System_Console__CreateOutputWriter ()
#5  0x00000000019fca43 in System_Console_System_Console___get_Error_g__EnsureInitialized_27_0 ()
#6  0x00000000018465b0 in S_P_DeveloperExperience_Console_Internal_Runtime_CompilerHelpers_LibraryInitializer___c___InitializeLibrary_b__0_0 ()
#7  0x0000000001108255 in S_P_CoreLib_System_RuntimeExceptionHelpers__FailFast_1 ()
#8  0x00000000011c354d in S_P_CoreLib_System_Globalization_GlobalizationMode_Settings___cctor ()
#9  0x00000000011b38d9 in S_P_CoreLib_System_Runtime_CompilerServices_ClassConstructorRunner__EnsureClassConstructorRun
    ()
#10 0x00000000011b37e9 in S_P_CoreLib_System_Runtime_CompilerServices_ClassConstructorRunner__CheckStaticClassConstructionReturnNonGCStaticBase ()
#11 0x0000000001149393 in S_P_CoreLib_System_Globalization_CultureData__CreateCultureWithInvariantData ()
#12 0x000000000114945a in S_P_CoreLib_System_Globalization_CultureData__get_Invariant ()
#13 0x000000000115dc5c in S_P_CoreLib_System_Globalization_TextInfo___cctor ()
#14 0x00000000011b38d9 in S_P_CoreLib_System_Runtime_CompilerServices_ClassConstructorRunner__EnsureClassConstructorRun
    ()
#15 0x00000000011b37d9 in S_P_CoreLib_System_Runtime_CompilerServices_ClassConstructorRunner__CheckStaticClassConstructionReturnGCStaticBase ()
#16 0x0000000001a0388e in System_Console_System_Text_EncodingHelper__GetCharset ()
#17 0x0000000001a037a9 in System_Console_System_Text_EncodingHelper__GetEncodingFromCharset ()
#18 0x00000000019fd729 in System_Console_System_ConsolePal__GetConsoleEncoding ()
#19 0x00000000019fc738 in System_Console_System_Console__get_OutputEncoding ()
#20 0x00000000019fc80b in System_Console_System_Console__CreateOutputWriter ()
#21 0x00000000019fc9a3 in System_Console_System_Console___get_Out_g__EnsureInitialized_25_0 ()
#22 0x00000000019fc8e0 in System_Console_System_Console__WriteLine_12 ()
#23 0x00000000017f88b2 in bflat_Program__Run ()
#24 0x00000000017fc572 in bflat_Program__Main ()
#25 0x0000000001dbf225 in bflat__Module___StartupCodeMain ()
#26 0x0000000000dec7ae in main ()

item.title: Issue: Selective reflection metadata generation - @hez2010
item.description:
Hello, I only need reflection for some specific types, setting IlcDisableReflection to false for the whole app would cause a major size regression.

Is there any way to emit reflection metadata selectively so that metadata are only generated for types I specified?

item.title: Issue: [NativeAOT] Best way to track down app size bloat? - @svengeance
item.description:
Hey all,

I'm looking for the recommended approach to track down exactly what is contributing to the large size of my app. I'm sitting at roughly 12MB right now with all optimizations enabled. After getting rid of everything that makes C# feel like home (MS.Ext.Logging, MS.Ext.DI, etc..) I've got two dependencies - FluentValidation and Serilog at this time. I am likely going to ax FV, but I'd prefer not to write my own logger.

(It may sound far-reaching but size is of utmost importance here, and I'm trying to stay close to that 1.4MB hello world where I can)

<DebuggerSupport>false</DebuggerSupport>
<EnableUnsafeBinaryFormatterSerialization>false</EnableUnsafeBinaryFormatterSerialization>
<EnableUnsafeUTF7Encoding>false</EnableUnsafeUTF7Encoding>
<EventSourceSupport>false</EventSourceSupport>
<HttpActivityPropagationSupport>false</HttpActivityPropagationSupport>
<UseSystemResourceKeys>true</UseSystemResourceKeys>

<IlcDisableUnhandledExceptionExperience>true</IlcDisableUnhandledExceptionExperience>
<IlcGenerateStackTraceData>false</IlcGenerateStackTraceData>
<IlcInvariantGlobalization>true</IlcInvariantGlobalization>
<IlcOptimizationPreference>Size</IlcOptimizationPreference>

<IlcFoldIdenticalMethodBodies>true</IlcFoldIdenticalMethodBodies>

<IlcDisableReflection>true</IlcDisableReflection>

<IlcGenerateMapFile>true</IlcGenerateMapFile>
<IlcGenerateMetadataLog>true</IlcGenerateMetadataLog>
<IlcGenerateDgmlFile>true</IlcGenerateDgmlFile>

As you can see on the bottom I've got the debug files enabled, however I am unable to reasonably read through them; the dgml file never loads in any viewer I've tried (> 100MB/100k nodes), and it's hard to track the causal relationships in the Mapfile.

So far to be honest the only real direction I've been able to get from these files in a small amount of time is searching for a dependency package and seeing how many lines are related.

If I must, I will find a way to parse these files and even produce some tooling around it. However, if any exists, I would appreciate some guidance.

Thanks all.

item.title: Issue: [NativeAOT] Mysql.Data reflection exception - @aizuon
item.description:
Opening a connection with a NativeAOT compiled library results in an exception.

Sample code:

using MySql.Data.MySqlClient;

namespace mysql_nativeaot_test
{
    public static class Program
    {
        private static void Main()
        {
            string connectionString =
                "SslMode=none;Server=127.0.0.1;Port=3306;Database=test;Uid=root;Pwd=1337;Pooling=true;";
            var connection = new MySqlConnection(connectionString);
            connection.Open();
        }
    }
}

Exception:

Unhandled Exception: System.TypeInitializationException: A type initializer threw an exception. To determine which type, inspect the InnerException's StackTrace property.
 ---> System.TypeInitializationException: A type initializer threw an exception. To determine which type, inspect the InnerException's StackTrace property.
 ---> System.Configuration.ConfigurationErrorsException: Configuration system failed to initialize
 ---> System.MissingMethodException: No parameterless constructor defined for this object.
   at System.ActivatorImplementation.CreateInstance(Type, Boolean) + 0xeb
   at System.Configuration.Internal.ConfigSystem.System.Configuration.Internal.IConfigSystem.Init(Type, Object[]) + 0x40
   at System.Configuration.ClientConfigurationSystem..ctor() + 0x47
   at System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager.EnsureConfigurationSystem() + 0x56
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager.EnsureConfigurationSystem() + 0xf1
   at System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager.PrepareConfigSystem() + 0x13
   at System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager.GetSection(String) + 0x20
   at MySql.Data.MySqlClient.MySqlConfiguration..cctor() + 0x11
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ClassConstructorRunner.EnsureClassConstructorRun(StaticClassConstructionContext*) + 0xc7
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ClassConstructorRunner.EnsureClassConstructorRun(StaticClassConstructionContext*) + 0x16c
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ClassConstructorRunner.CheckStaticClassConstructionReturnGCStaticBase(StaticClassConstructionContext*, Object) + 0xd
   at MySql.Data.MySqlClient.Replication.ReplicationManager..cctor() + 0x78
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ClassConstructorRunner.EnsureClassConstructorRun(StaticClassConstructionContext*) + 0xc7
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ClassConstructorRunner.EnsureClassConstructorRun(StaticClassConstructionContext*) + 0x16c
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.ClassConstructorRunner.CheckStaticClassConstructionReturnNonGCStaticBase(StaticClassConstructionContext*, IntPtr) + 0xd
   at MySql.Data.MySqlClient.Replication.ReplicationManager.IsReplicationGroup(String) + 0x10
   at MySql.Data.MySqlClient.MySqlConnection.Open() + 0x570
   at mysql-nativeaot-test!<BaseAddress>+0xcc2bd6

Mysql.Data uses System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager which calls System.Configuration.ClientConfigurationSystem constructor at some point:

internal ClientConfigurationSystem()
{
    IConfigSystem configSystem = new ConfigSystem();
    configSystem.Init(typeof(ClientConfigurationHost), null, null);

    _configHost = configSystem.Host;
    _configRoot = configSystem.Root;

    _configRoot.ConfigRemoved += OnConfigRemoved;

    _isAppConfigHttp = ((IInternalConfigHostPaths)_configHost).IsAppConfigHttp;
}

In this constructor, System.Configuration.Internal.ConfigSystem.Init() is called:

void IConfigSystem.Init(Type typeConfigHost, params object[] hostInitParams)
{
    _configRoot = new InternalConfigRoot();

    // Create the requested host and wrap in ImplicitMachineConfigHost so we can
    // stub in a simple machine.config if needed.
    IInternalConfigHost host = (IInternalConfigHost)TypeUtil.CreateInstance(typeConfigHost);
    _configHost = new ImplicitMachineConfigHost(host);

    _configRoot.Init(_configHost, isDesignTime: false);
    _configHost.Init(_configRoot, hostInitParams);
}

In this function, TypeUtil is used to create an instance of System.Configuration.ClientConfigurationHost which internally calls Activator.CreateInstance(), but Activator.CreateInstance() can't find System.Configuration.ClientConfigurationHost's parameterless constructor, which exists in https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/blob/main/src/libraries/System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager/src/System/Configuration/ClientConfigurationHost.cs as:

internal ClientConfigurationHost()
{
    Host = new InternalConfigHost();
}

The constructor being internal is not the issue as Activator.CreateInstance() is called with nonPublic parameter set to true.

Using the rd.xml below to preserve the unfound constructor doesn't fix the issue:

<Directives xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/netfx/2013/01/metadata">
  <Application>
    <Assembly Name="System.Configuration.ConfigurationManager">
      <Type Name="System.Configuration.ClientConfigurationHost" Dynamic="Required All" />
    </Assembly>
  </Application>
</Directives>

item.title: Issue: Question: NativeAOT-LLVM: Argument order in GenTreeCall.Operands() - @yowl
item.description:
I naively thought that the Operands method on GenTreeCall would be in the IL order but looking at the IR for https://github.com/dotnet/runtimelab/blob/a21e41e80ec07dcea7b18a6b1b8c90220f54af31/src/libraries/System.Private.CoreLib/src/System/OutOfMemoryException.cs#L15-L21

------------ BB01 [000..01A) (return), preds={} succs={}
               [000016] ------------                 IL_OFFSET void   IL offset: 0x0
N002 ( 14,  2) [000001] --CXG-------         t1 =    CALL      ref    System.SR.get_Arg_OutOfMemoryException
                                                  /--*  t1     ref
N004 ( 18,  5) [000011] DA-XG-----L-              *  STORE_LCL_VAR ref    V02 tmp1
N005 (  3,  2) [000012] ------------        t12 =    LCL_VAR   ref    V02 tmp1
N006 (  3,  2) [000000] ------------         t0 =    LCL_VAR   ref    V00 this
                                                  /--*  t12    ref    arg1 in rdx
                                                  +--*  t0     ref    this in rcx
N007 ( 41, 15) [000002] --CXG-------              *  CALL      void   System.SystemException..ctor
N001 (  1,  1) [000003] ------------                 NO_OP     void
N001 (  1,  1) [000004] ------------                 NO_OP     void
N003 (  3,  2) [000005] ------------         t5 =    LCL_VAR   ref    V00 this
N004 (  1,  4) [000006] ------------         t6 =    CNS_INT   int    -0x7FF8FFF2
                                                  /--*  t5     ref    this in rcx
                                                  +--*  t6     int    arg1 in rdx
N005 ( 18, 11) [000007] --CXG-------              *  CALL      void   System.Exception.set_HResult
N001 (  1,  1) [000008] ------------                 NO_OP     void
N001 (  0,  0) [000009] ------------                 RETURN    void

I can see that the 2nd and 3rd CALLs have this in different positions. That doesn't matter I suppose when the args are in registers. If I want them in IL order do I need something like

   fgArgTabEntry* curArgTabEntry = gtArgEntryByLateArgIndex(call, lateArgIndex);
  // use curArgTabEntry->argNum

Or is there a more correct way?

item.title: Issue: Weird Linq Expression behaviour in NativeAOT - @ryancheung
item.description:
I have a project depends on LiteDB.

The minimal repro code:

namespace ConsoleApp1
{
    public sealed class KeyBindInfo
    {
        public int Id { get; set; }
        public string Category { get; set; }

        public KeyBindAction Action { get; set; }
    }

    public enum KeyBindAction
    {
        None,

        ConfigWindow,
    }

    class Program
    {
        public static ILiteCollection<KeyBindInfo> KeyBinds;

        static void Main(string[] args)
        {
            var mapper = new BsonMapper();

            mapper.Entity<KeyBindInfo>()
                .Id(x => x.Id, true);
            var UserDB = new LiteDatabase($"Filename=User.db;Mode=Exclusive", mapper);

            KeyBinds = UserDB.GetCollection<KeyBindInfo>();

            // This is working!
            var a = KeyBinds.Exists(x => x.Action == KeyBindAction.ConfigWindow);

            // This is NOT working!
            var tmp = KeyBindAction.ConfigWindow;
            a = KeyBinds.Exists(x => x.Action == tmp); // Crash in NativeAOT
            Console.WriteLine($"next............");

            Console.WriteLine("Done.");
        }
    }
}

The full repro project: https://github.com/ryancheung/NativeAOTRepro

item.title: Issue: Question: GenTree identifier - @yowl
item.description:
In NativeAOT-LLVM the gtTreeID is used as a key to lookup LLVM Values, e.g. GT_STOREIND is implemented with this

void importStoreInd(llvm::IRBuilder<>& builder, GenTreeStoreInd* storeIndOp)
{
    Value* address = getTreeIdValue(storeIndOp->Addr());
    Value* toStore = getTreeIdValue(storeIndOp->Data());

where getTreeIdValue is looking up the Value* based on the gtTreeID. This is not going to work in Release mode as gtTreeID is #ifed in only for DEBUG. Where a GenTree is returned in cases like the one above, Addr() and Data() is it the same struct as when the node is "created", i.e. with this IR

N005 (  1,  4) [000007] H-----------         t7 =    CNS_INT(h) ref    0x420C08 [ICON_STR_HDL]
                                                  /--*  t3     byref
                                                  +--*  t7     ref

The GenTree for the CNS_INT, would it have the same address as the result of Data() and therefore can be relied upon like a GetHashCode() in .Net or does the compiler create different GenTree structs to represent the same node ?

item.title: Issue: Marshal.ReleaseComObject in NativeAOT - @kant2002
item.description: I would like to understand how https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/54317 will affect NativeAOT. I understand decision behind answer, but slitly disappointed that WPF, and other libraries out of reach even with COM support in NativeAOT. Maybe I miss some idea?

item.title: Issue: Correct way of finding the missing type for the rd.xml - @Fabi
item.description:
I know that isn't really a supported scenario at the moment, but I tried getting an obfuscator working with NativeAOT (Eazfuscator).

It starts with Unhandled Exception: System.PlatformNotSupportedException: Operation is not supported on this platform. at System.Reflection.Assembly.GetCallingAssembly() but that can be worked around by using the Switch.System.Reflection.Assembly.SimulatedCallingAssembly switch.

However after that I'm hard stuck with

   at System.Reflection.Runtime.TypeInfos.NativeFormat.NativeFormatRuntimeNamedTypeInfo.get_MetadataToken() + 0x47
   at <Eazfuscator ©>.A._x_C._x_lll111l11ll1111l1(Type) + 0x25
   at <Eazfuscator ©>.A._x_C._x_A() + 0x260
   at <Eazfuscator ©>.S._x_A._x_l1llll11ll11lll1l(Int32, Boolean) + 0x920
   at <Eazfuscator ©>.S._x_A._x_A(Int32) + 0x5f
   at <Eazfuscator © CI>.B._x_a0._x_eI1pUD01rkaHNNk7fY9jPzDJE(Boolean) + 0x47
   at <Eazfuscator © CI>.B._x_a0._x_a1() + 0x14
   at NativeObfuscationTest._x_Program._x_Main(String[]) + 0x24
   at NativeObfuscationTest!<BaseAddress>+0x32a6c7
   at NativeObfuscationTest!<BaseAddress>+0x32a755

I tried adding a simple rd.xml for the Eazfuscator types like in here but that does not help.

Is there any way to properly see what is missing here? It;s a simple Hello World default console app

item.title: Issue: Implement GetMethodWithSameMetadataDefinitionAs for NativeAOT - @jkotas
item.description:
See https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/pull/53704

We will get the generic implementation on System.Type that should work ok, but we should be able to have better more efficient one.

cc @MichalStrehovsky
